Body shrinks to: build `ExecuteRequest{Prompt, Model, WorkDir, Permissions, Effort, Timeout, StallPolicy}`, call `service.Execute(ctx, req)`, drain events. **All of:** provider construction, compactor wiring, tool registration, prompt building, session opening — moves into agentlib internals. The stall/circuit-breaker logic at 200-318 needs an upstream home — see "open structural decisions" below. |

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:501:{"acceptance":"ddx init in a fresh scratch dir creates exactly .claude/skills/ddx/, .agents/skills/ddx/, and .ddx/skills/ddx/ (no ddx-bead, ddx-run, etc.). In a scratch dir pre-seeded with .claude/skills/ddx-run/ and .claude/skills/ddx-doctor/, ddx init removes those two dirs and creates only .claude/skills/ddx/. Given an AGENTS.md with user content both before the start marker and after the end marker, ddx init updates the block between markers and preserves all user content outside markers. Running ddx init a second time does not duplicate the DDx block. cd cli && go test ./cmd -run TestInit passes with updated assertions covering stale-dir cleanup and AGENTS.md merge.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-17T22:25:52Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1484055","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:07:24.237048426Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-98bd0e32","depends_on_id":"ddx-086fc990","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:08:08Z"}],"description":"Update cli/cmd/init.go to handle both fresh projects and upgrades from older DDx versions.

Changes required:
1. Copy skills/ddx/ (whole directory tree — SKILL.md + reference/ + evals/) from the embedded FS into the target project:
     .claude/skills/ddx/ (for Claude Code)
     .agents/skills/ddx/ (for Codex and other agentskills.io consumers)
     .ddx/skills/ddx/    (project-local copy)
   Real files, not symlinks — symlinks break after git clone on a fresh machine.
2. Remove stale ddx-prefixed skill directories from prior DDx versions before copying the new one. Known stale dirs: ddx-bead, ddx-run, ddx-agent, ddx-review, ddx-status, ddx-doctor, ddx-install, ddx-release. Check each of .claude/skills/, .agents/skills/, .ddx/skills/. Do NOT touch third-party skills — only the known ddx-prefixed allowlist.
3. AGENTS.md: merge, do not clobber. Use marker-delimited injection like the existing CLAUDE.md meta-prompt injector (see cli/internal/metaprompt/). Wrap DDx content in:
     <!-- DDX-AGENTS:START -->
     (DDx block)
     <!-- DDX-AGENTS:END -->
   If AGENTS.md does not exist: create with the block. If exists with markers: replace block content between markers, preserve everything outside. If exists without markers: insert at end. Re-running ddx init must not duplicate the block.
4. AGENTS.md block content:
   - No 'symlinks' wording (files are real now).
   - One-line pointer: 'This project uses DDx. Use the

Document-Driven Development eXperience

DDx is a toolkit for AI-assisted development that helps you:

• Share templates, prompts, and patterns across projects
• Maintain consistent development practices
• Integrate AI tooling seamlessly
• Contribute improvements back to the community

Get started:
  ddx init          Initialize DDx in your project
  ddx list          See available resources
  ddx doctor        Check installation and diagnose issues

More information:
  Documentation: https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx
  Issues & Support: https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/issues

Usage:
  ddx [command]

Available Commands:
  agent       Invoke AI agents with harness dispatch, quorum, and session logging
  bead        Manage beads (portable work items)
  checkpoint  Create, list, or restore named git checkpoints
  completion  Generate completion script
  config      Configure DDx settings
  doc         Document dependency graph and staleness tracking
  doctor      Check installation health and diagnose issues
  exec        Validate and run DDx execution definitions
  help        Help about any command
  init        Initialize DDx in current project
  install     Install a package or resource
  installed   List installed packages
  jq          Process JSON with jq expressions
  list        List available DDx resources
  log         Show DDX asset history
  metric      Validate and run metric definitions
  metrics     Show process metrics derived from beads and agent sessions
  outdated    List installed packages with available updates
  persona     Manage AI personas for consistent interactions
  prompts     Manage AI prompts
  search      Search available packages
  server      Start the DDx HTTP and MCP server
  skills      Validate bundled skill packages
  status      Show version and status information
  uninstall   Remove an installed package
  update      Update DDx CLI or installed plugins
  upgrade     Upgrade DDx to the latest version
  verify      Verify installed package integrity
  version     Show version information
  work        Work the bead execution queue

Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
  -h, --help                       help for ddx
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

Status of each AC against current HEAD (97b75512):

1. "Ladder type + Next() method in cli/internal/agent/escalation/"
   PRESENT: cli/internal/agent/escalation/ladder.go defines:
     - type Ladder
     - func NewLadder([]agentlib.ModelInfo) *Ladder
     - func (l *Ladder) Next(actualPower int) (int, error)
     - func (l *Ladder) Tiers() []int
   No vendor names appear; ladder reasons over Power tiers only.

2. "Unit tests cover all transitions"
   PRESENT: cli/internal/agent/escalation/ladder_test.go covers:
     - Tiers distinct/ascending and ignoring unrated (Power<=0) models
     - Next stepping through all-viable tiers, including strictly-greater
     - Exhaustion (ErrLadderExhausted) on top tier and empty/nil ladders
     - Skip-tier returning *NoViableProviderError, then bumping past it
     - All-non-viable catalog exhausting via repeated skips
     - Partial availability (Available xor AutoRoutable) counted non-viable

3. "Skip-tier returns typed NoViableProviderError"
   PRESENT: ladder.go declares `type NoViableProviderError struct{ Floor int }`
   with Error() method; Next returns &NoViableProviderError{Floor: tier}
   when the next tier above actualPower has no viable model.
   TestLadder_Next_SkipTierReturnsNoViableProviderError uses errors.As to
   confirm the typed error and Floor value.

4. "Tests assert ladder uses RoutingActual.Power as input for next-floor
   computation"
   PRESENT: TestLadder_Next_UsesRoutingActualPower constructs a stand-in
   RoutingActual record, passes prev.Power directly to Next, asserts the
   returned floor (90 from prev.Power=70), and additionally swaps
   Provider/Model/Harness to confirm Power is the sole signal.

which is an ancestor of current HEAD. The prior REVIEW:BLOCK note on this

rationale: Bead ddx-51ac997b: no changes needed — all acceptance criteria already
satisfied by code already merged into HEAD.

Status of each AC against current HEAD (97b75512):

1. "Ladder type + Next() method in cli/internal/agent/escalation/"
   PRESENT: cli/internal/agent/escalation/ladder.go defines:
     - type Ladder
     - func NewLadder([]agentlib.ModelInfo) *Ladder
     - func (l *Ladder) Next(actualPower int) (int, error)
     - func (l *Ladder) Tiers() []int
   No vendor names appear; ladder reasons over Power tiers only.

2. "Unit tests cover all transitions"
   PRESENT: cli/internal/agent/escalation/ladder_test.go covers:
     - Tiers distinct/ascending and ignoring unrated (Power<=0) models
     - Next stepping through all-viable tiers, including strictly-greater
     - Exhaustion (ErrLadderExhausted) on top tier and empty/nil ladders
     - Skip-tier returning *NoViableProviderError, then bumping past it
     - All-non-viable catalog exhausting via repeated skips
     - Partial availability (Available xor AutoRoutable) counted non-viable

3. "Skip-tier returns typed NoViableProviderError"
   PRESENT: ladder.go declares `type NoViableProviderError struct{ Floor int }`
   with Error() method; Next returns &NoViableProviderError{Floor: tier}
   when the next tier above actualPower has no viable model.
   TestLadder_Next_SkipTierReturnsNoViableProviderError uses errors.As to
   confirm the typed error and Floor value.

4. "Tests assert ladder uses RoutingActual.Power as input for next-floor
   computation"
   PRESENT: TestLadder_Next_UsesRoutingActualPower constructs a stand-in
   RoutingActual record, passes prev.Power directly to Next, asserts the
   returned floor (90 from prev.Power=70), and additionally swaps
   Provider/Model/Harness to confirm Power is the sole signal.

2. Remove stale ddx-prefixed skill directories from prior DDx versions before copying the new one. Known stale dirs: ddx-bead, ddx-run, ddx-agent, ddx-review, ddx-status, ddx-doctor, ddx-install, ddx-release. Check each of .claude/skills/, .agents/skills/, .ddx/skills/.    If AGENTS.md does not exist: create with the block. If exists with markers: replace block content between markers, preserve everything outside. If exists without markers: insert at end. Re-running ddx init must not duplicate the block.

Document-Driven Development eXperience

DDx is a toolkit for AI-assisted development that helps you:

• Share templates, prompts, and patterns across projects
• Maintain consistent development practices
• Integrate AI tooling seamlessly
• Contribute improvements back to the community

Get started:
  ddx init          Initialize DDx in your project
  ddx list          See available resources
  ddx doctor        Check installation and diagnose issues

More information:
  Documentation: https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx
  Issues & Support: https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/issues

Usage:
  ddx [command]

Available Commands:
  agent       Invoke AI agents with harness dispatch, quorum, and session logging
  bead        Manage beads (portable work items)
  checkpoint  Create, list, or restore named git checkpoints
  completion  Generate completion script
  config      Configure DDx settings
  doc         Document dependency graph and staleness tracking
  doctor      Check installation health and diagnose issues
  exec        Validate and run DDx execution definitions
  help        Help about any command
  init        Initialize DDx in current project
  install     Install a package or resource
  installed   List installed packages
  jq          Process JSON with jq expressions
  list        List available DDx resources
  log         Show DDX asset history
  metric      Validate and run metric definitions
  metrics     Show process metrics derived from beads and agent sessions
  outdated    List installed packages with available updates
  persona     Manage AI personas for consistent interactions
  prompts     Manage AI prompts
  search      Search available packages
  server      Start the DDx HTTP and MCP server
  skills      Validate bundled skill packages
  status      Show version and status information
  uninstall   Remove an installed package
  update      Update DDx CLI or installed plugins
  upgrade     Upgrade DDx to the latest version
  verify      Verify installed package integrity
  version     Show version information
  work        Work the bead execution queue

Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
  -h, --help                       help for ddx
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

.ddx/beads.jsonl:1103:{"acceptance":"1. library/checks/go-production-reachability/ exists with: README.md (usage), check.yaml (template), implementation (binary or script).
2. Implementation reads DIFF_BASE/DIFF_HEAD/PROJECT_ROOT/EVIDENCE_DIR from env; computes new symbols from diff; runs deadcode-rooted reachability; writes result.json conforming to REACH-PROTO schema.
3. // wiring:pending <bead-id> annotation parsed; bead existence + open-status verified against .ddx/beads.jsonl; invalid annotation = violation.
4. _test.go files excluded from both root selection and reachability calculation (so a unit-test-only reference does NOT satisfy the check).
5. Self-test: synthetic project with one unwired exported function returns status=block with that symbol in violations.
6. Self-test: same project with the function wired into cmd handler returns status=pass.
7. Self-test: same project with // wiring:pending annotation citing an open bead returns status=pass; annotation citing a closed/missing bead returns status=block.
8. This project installs it: .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml committed; ddx ac run on any current bead succeeds (or BLOCKs with the known unwired functions — see REACH-BACKFILL for resolution).
9. cd cli && go test green; lefthook pre-commit passes.
10. Evidence file at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/production-reachability.json shows the check ran and its findings.","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T12:30:48Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"463338","closing_commit_sha":"3e58316310e9cf2122afed341ec0ca0a63b6f66d","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:35:10.781525398Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-ea76e1b8","depends_on_id":"ddx-a946c744","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:35:13Z"}],"description":"Ship the Go-specific production-reachability check as a library resource conforming to the REACH-PROTO check protocol. NOT installed by default for arbitrary projects (Go-opinion would leak through ddx init). This project (ddx itself) opts in by adding .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml.

DESIGN
- Location: library/checks/go-production-reachability/ (alongside library/personas/, library/patterns/)
- Implementation: a small Go binary or shell script invoking golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode rooted at configurable entry packages.
- Default entry roots for ddx self-use: cli/cmd/..., cli/internal/server/graphql/..., cli/internal/server/http/...
- Logic:
  1. Read DIFF_BASE..DIFF_HEAD from env. Compute set of new Go symbols (functions, methods, types) added by the diff via AST parse of changed files.
  2. Run deadcode (or callgraph) rooted at the configured entry packages, EXCLUDING _test.go from roots and from reachability.
  3. For each new symbol not reachable from any entry root → violation { file, line, symbol, kind: "unreachable", detail: "no path from entry roots: <list>" }
  4. Recognize // wiring:pending <bead-id> annotation as escape hatch for individual symbols. The check verifies the named bead exists and is open via local beads.jsonl read; otherwise the annotation is rejected.
  5. Write ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/production-reachability.json with the protocol-defined schema.
- Config: library/checks/go-production-reachability/check.yaml template that operators copy to .ddx/checks/ and customize entry_roots for their project.

DOGFOOD
- This project installs it: write .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml referencing the library check + cli's entry roots.
- The next ddx try on any bead touching cli/**/*.go will exercise the check.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Auto-install during ddx init (would leak Go-opinion). Operators opt in manually or via ddx install check (future, separate bead).
- Coverage-based "exercised via CLI" check (entered_via flavor). Static reachability is sufficient for the immediate problem.
- Python/TS/Rust analogues. Future contributions; protocol supports them.

.ddx/executions/20260503T123048-397071dc/manifest.json:13:    "description": "Ship the Go-specific production-reachability check as a library resource conforming to the REACH-PROTO check protocol. NOT installed by default for arbitrary projects (Go-opinion would leak through ddx init). This project (ddx itself) opts in by adding .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml.

DESIGN
- Location: library/checks/go-production-reachability/ (alongside library/personas/, library/patterns/)
- Implementation: a small Go binary or shell script invoking golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode rooted at configurable entry packages.
- Default entry roots for ddx self-use: cli/cmd/..., cli/internal/server/graphql/..., cli/internal/server/http/...
- Logic:
  1. Read DIFF_BASE..DIFF_HEAD from env. Compute set of new Go symbols (functions, methods, types) added by the diff via AST parse of changed files.
  2. Run deadcode (or callgraph) rooted at the configured entry packages, EXCLUDING _test.go from roots and from reachability.
  3. For each new symbol not reachable from any entry root → violation { file, line, symbol, kind: "unreachable", detail: "no path from entry roots: <list>" }
  4. Recognize // wiring:pending <bead-id> annotation as escape hatch for individual symbols. The check verifies the named bead exists and is open via local beads.jsonl read; otherwise the annotation is rejected.
  5. Write ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/production-reachability.json with the protocol-defined schema.
- Config: library/checks/go-production-reachability/check.yaml template that operators copy to .ddx/checks/ and customize entry_roots for their project.

DOGFOOD
- This project installs it: write .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml referencing the library check + cli's entry roots.
- The next ddx try on any bead touching cli/**/*.go will exercise the check.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Auto-install during ddx init (would leak Go-opinion). Operators opt in manually or via ddx install check (future, separate bead).
- Coverage-based "exercised via CLI" check (entered_via flavor). Static reachability is sufficient for the immediate problem.
- Python/TS/Rust analogues. Future contributions; protocol supports them.

DEP: REACH-PROTO (above)",

.ddx/executions/20260503T124415-1a86a675/prompt.md:102:+    "description": "Ship the Go-specific production-reachability check as a library resource conforming to the REACH-PROTO check protocol. NOT installed by default for arbitrary projects (Go-opinion would leak through ddx init). This project (ddx itself) opts in by adding .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml.

DESIGN
- Location: library/checks/go-production-reachability/ (alongside library/personas/, library/patterns/)
- Implementation: a small Go binary or shell script invoking golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode rooted at configurable entry packages.
- Default entry roots for ddx self-use: cli/cmd/..., cli/internal/server/graphql/..., cli/internal/server/http/...
- Logic:
  1. Read DIFF_BASE..DIFF_HEAD from env. Compute set of new Go symbols (functions, methods, types) added by the diff via AST parse of changed files.
  2. Run deadcode (or callgraph) rooted at the configured entry packages, EXCLUDING _test.go from roots and from reachability.
  3. For each new symbol not reachable from any entry root → violation { file, line, symbol, kind: "unreachable", detail: "no path from entry roots: <list>" }
  4. Recognize // wiring:pending <bead-id> annotation as escape hatch for individual symbols. The check verifies the named bead exists and is open via local beads.jsonl read; otherwise the annotation is rejected.
  5. Write ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/production-reachability.json with the protocol-defined schema.
- Config: library/checks/go-production-reachability/check.yaml template that operators copy to .ddx/checks/ and customize entry_roots for their project.

DOGFOOD
- This project installs it: write .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml referencing the library check + cli's entry roots.
- The next ddx try on any bead touching cli/**/*.go will exercise the check.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Auto-install during ddx init (would leak Go-opinion). Operators opt in manually or via ddx install check (future, separate bead).
- Coverage-based "exercised via CLI" check (entered_via flavor). Static reachability is sufficient for the immediate problem.
- Python/TS/Rust analogues. Future contributions; protocol supports them.

DEP: REACH-PROTO (above)",

.ddx/executions/20260503T025720-bb59ccca/manifest.json:13:    "description": "Add a language-agnostic check protocol to ddx so projects can declare mechanical pre-merge gates that ddx try evaluates before any worktree is merged back. Solves the recurring "implemented but not wired" failure mode at the platform layer rather than per-project.

CONTEXT
- Audit at /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md surfaced 4 functions with tests but no production caller; reviewer didn't catch it because the diff "looked right" and tests passed.
- ac_structural_check.go (cli/internal/agent/) is the existing seed: parses claims from AC prose for test_name/file_deleted/field_removed. It can't catch the unwired case because a unit test naming the function satisfies the claim vacuously.
- Prior plan considered embedding Go-specific call-graph analysis directly in cli/internal/agent/. Rejected as a boundary violation (CLAUDE.md: "DDx provides primitives; HELIX and others provide opinions"). Each language ecosystem has its own dead-code tooling (Go: deadcode/callgraph; Python: vulture; TS: ts-prune/knip; Rust: cargo-udeps).

DESIGN — protocol (declarative, plugin-based)
- Discovery: .ddx/checks/*.yaml each declares one check: { name, command, when: pre_merge, applies_to: { paths: [globs] | always, labels: [...] } }
- Inputs to check command (env vars): BEAD_ID, DIFF_BASE, DIFF_HEAD, PROJECT_ROOT, EVIDENCE_DIR, RUN_ID
- Output contract: check writes ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json with schema { status: pass|block|error, message: string, violations: [{file, line, symbol, kind, detail}] }
- Exit code 0 = ran-to-completion (status field is authoritative); non-zero = check itself errored (treated as BLOCK with status=error)
- Hook point: pre_merge — runs in the parent (after worker's worktree work is done, BEFORE the merge-back into integration branch). BLOCK aborts the merge; the worktree stays preserved per existing conventions for human resolution.
- Bypass: bead-level annotation checks_bypass: [{name: <check>, reason: <text>, bead: <bead-id>}] recorded in events. No CLI flag — bypass must be tracked.
- Standalone: ddx ac run <bead-id> [--check <name>] runs the same checks locally so devs can iterate without spawning a full try attempt.

WHY pre_merge (NOT pre_close in the worker)
- User directive: "We can't afford to merge this kind of dead code." Worker-side checks could be bypassed by a worker that ignores BLOCK; parent-side gate cannot.
- Worker still gets feedback via ddx ac run for local iteration.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Multiple lifecycle hook points beyond pre_merge (add later if needed).
- Check dependencies / ordering (independent + parallel until proven insufficient).
- Plugin manifests, capability flags, version negotiation.
- Auto-discovery beyond .ddx/checks/*.yaml.
- Retry / timeout policy (project owns; checks should be fast).
- Any specific language check (separate beads).

OUT OF SCOPE: REACH-GO (the Go-specific deadcode-based check) and REACH-BACKFILL (the systematic application across cli/) are separate beads that depend on this one.

FILES (anticipated)
- cli/internal/checks/protocol.go — config schema + JSON I/O types
- cli/internal/checks/loader.go — read .ddx/checks/*.yaml
- cli/internal/checks/runner.go — subprocess invocation, env injection, output parsing, evidence dir mgmt
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go — wire pre_merge call before merge step; BLOCK aborts merge
- cli/cmd/ac.go — ddx ac run subcommand
- cli/internal/checks/*_test.go — table-driven tests for protocol contract",

.ddx/executions/20260503T030952-1063db26/manifest.json:13:    "description": "Ship the Go-specific production-reachability check as a library resource conforming to the REACH-PROTO check protocol. NOT installed by default for arbitrary projects (Go-opinion would leak through ddx init). This project (ddx itself) opts in by adding .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml.

DESIGN
- Location: library/checks/go-production-reachability/ (alongside library/personas/, library/patterns/)
- Implementation: a small Go binary or shell script invoking golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode rooted at configurable entry packages.
- Default entry roots for ddx self-use: cli/cmd/..., cli/internal/server/graphql/..., cli/internal/server/http/...
- Logic:
  1. Read DIFF_BASE..DIFF_HEAD from env. Compute set of new Go symbols (functions, methods, types) added by the diff via AST parse of changed files.
  2. Run deadcode (or callgraph) rooted at the configured entry packages, EXCLUDING _test.go from roots and from reachability.
  3. For each new symbol not reachable from any entry root → violation { file, line, symbol, kind: "unreachable", detail: "no path from entry roots: <list>" }
  4. Recognize // wiring:pending <bead-id> annotation as escape hatch for individual symbols. The check verifies the named bead exists and is open via local beads.jsonl read; otherwise the annotation is rejected.
  5. Write ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/production-reachability.json with the protocol-defined schema.
- Config: library/checks/go-production-reachability/check.yaml template that operators copy to .ddx/checks/ and customize entry_roots for their project.

DOGFOOD
- This project installs it: write .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml referencing the library check + cli's entry roots.
- The next ddx try on any bead touching cli/**/*.go will exercise the check.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Auto-install during ddx init (would leak Go-opinion). Operators opt in manually or via ddx install check (future, separate bead).
- Coverage-based "exercised via CLI" check (entered_via flavor). Static reachability is sufficient for the immediate problem.
- Python/TS/Rust analogues. Future contributions; protocol supports them.

DEP: REACH-PROTO (above)",
.ddx/executions/20260503T030952-1063db26/manifest.json:14:    "acceptance": "1. library/checks/go-production-reachability/ exists with: README.md (usage), check.yaml (template), implementation (binary or script).
2. Implementation reads DIFF_BASE/DIFF_HEAD/PROJECT_ROOT/EVIDENCE_DIR from env; computes new symbols from diff; runs deadcode-rooted reachability; writes result.json conforming to REACH-PROTO schema.
3. // wiring:pending <bead-id> annotation parsed; bead existence + open-status verified against .ddx/beads.jsonl; invalid annotation = violation.
4. _test.go files excluded from both root selection and reachability calculation (so a unit-test-only reference does NOT satisfy the check).
5. Self-test: synthetic project with one unwired exported function returns status=block with that symbol in violations.
6. Self-test: same project with the function wired into cmd handler returns status=pass.
7. Self-test: same project with // wiring:pending annotation citing an open bead returns status=pass; annotation citing a closed/missing bead returns status=block.
8. This project installs it: .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml committed; ddx ac run on any current bead succeeds (or BLOCKs with the known unwired functions — see REACH-BACKFILL for resolution).
9. cd cli && go test green; lefthook pre-commit passes.
10. Evidence file at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/production-reachability.json shows the check ran and its findings.",

If the flag is due to an unrooted constructor or a wrapper-only path, I’ll wire it from `main()`-reachable code rather than deleting something still used.

The first read failed because I used the wrong worktree path. I’m re-reading from the actual directory now and then I’ll trace the constructor through `main()` to see whether this is just an entry-point wiring issue.
